name: pyft-wheel-gil-preflight
description: Fail CI when a free-threaded Python wheel import silently re-enables the GIL.
targetUsers: Maintainers who release C, C++, or Rust native extension wheels for free-threaded CPython 3.14 and later.
jobToBeDone: Inspect every native module in a built wheel with a compatible free-threaded interpreter and fail before release if an import re-enables the GIL.
distributionPath: Checksum-verified reproducible Linux and macOS archives, go install, and an offline source-built composite GitHub Action pinned by full commit SHA.
successMetric: Within 30 days, one unrelated native-extension repository uses the checker to catch a real missing GIL declaration before release and shows the module or release fix.

demandEvidence.independenceKey: Triton native module enables GIL on import

alternatives.name: CPython 3.14 RuntimeWarning, sys._is_gil_enabled, and -W error
alternatives.gap: Detects a known module when manually wired into a test but does not enumerate and isolate every native module in a built wheel.

alternatives.name: packaging 26.2 and cibuildwheel 4.1.0
alternatives.gap: Accepted the cp314t tag and selected the free-threaded build while leaving the post-import GIL invariant to a custom test command.

alternatives.name: pytest-freethreaded 0.1.0
alternatives.gap: Verified that the session started with the GIL disabled, then allowed the controlled bad extension import to warn and exit successfully.

alternatives.name: auditwheel 6.7.0 and abi3audit 0.0.26
alternatives.gap: Accepted or skipped the controlled cp314t binary without enforcing the module GIL declaration or the post-import GIL state.

duplicateSearch.summary: Installed exact alternative versions and ran the same valid bad wheel; tag, build, binary audit, and ordinary test paths all remained false-green while this checker returned PGP001.

differentiation: An offline built-wheel preflight that deterministically enumerates native modules, imports each in a bounded fresh process, and enforces the post-import GIL state without becoming a general ABI or thread-safety scanner.
